headline: Strong spending discipline, but 3 of 4 goals critically behind.
observations: Spending ratio 0.31 is excellent — today €20 vs €64.99 daily average — but income data shows €0 this month against €84.99 outflows.
Tokyo Trip needs €2,750 in 1 month (€2,750/month required); currently only €1,250 of €4,000 saved — unreachable at current pace.
Emergency Fund and Investment Starter both off-track; Emergency needs €565.61/month for 7 months, but no deposits visible this month.

goals.name: Tokyo Trip 2025
goals.note: €1,250/€4,000 saved; needs €2,750 in 1 month — impossible without major income.

goals.name: Emergency Fund
goals.note: €3,240.75/€7,200; €565.61/month needed; no contributions recorded this month.

web_insights.summary: NVDA +136% from €460 buy price to €1,087.50; significant unrealized gain warrants review of position size and profit-taking strategy.
AAPL +19% from €178.50 to €212.35; steady growth, monitor for sector headwinds.
VWCE.DE +5.7% from €115.20 to €121.80; stable diversified holding.

suggestions: Address income crisis: €0 recorded income this month but €84.99 spent. Verify income deposits are being tracked; if salary/wage is delayed, contact employer. Without income, all three failing goals cannot be funded.
Consider trimming NVDA position (up 136% from €460→€1,087.50): lock in €627.50/share gain on 1 share (~€628 realized), redirect to Emergency Fund shortfall (€3,959.25 needed).
Tokyo Trip is 1 month away with €2,750 gap: either postpone to next year and reallocate €1,250 to Emergency Fund, or secure €2,750 upfront (gift, bonus, loan) — current savings pace will not work.
advice: Clarify income status immediately. If salary/deposits are on hold or delayed, three goals will fail. If income is normal, investigate why zero appears in monthly_summary; a tracking issue may hide deposits. Then decide: reallocate NVDA gains to close Emergency Fund gap, or postpone/cancel Tokyo Trip to free €2,750 for critical reserves.
